summary: Add the SERP provider interface, deterministic mock provider, provider factory, and focused tests.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable SerpProvider interface exists with typed search input and normalized result fields aligned with the architecture docs.
A deterministic MockSerpProvider returns stable SERP-like results for keyword, country, language, and limit inputs without network calls, secrets, environment variables, or paid services.
A provider factory or index module exports the mock provider and fails fast for unsupported provider names without silently calling external services.
Focused automated tests cover deterministic output, limit handling, and required result shape.
README documents the mock provider slice and any new test command added to package scripts.
No scan orchestration, database writes, API route, LLM client, real SERP provider, cron, UI, auth, or deployment work is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/03_technical_architecture.md provider adapter guidance and docs/11_development_plan.md recommended execution order after Radar Task CRUD.
systems_architect is required because this introduces a service boundary and future provider contract.
qa_reviewer remains the independent acceptance reviewer.
Keep this task to provider interface/mock/test infrastructure only; scan orchestration and persistence belong to a later task.
non_goals: No SearchRun, KeywordCandidate, SerpResult, or Opportunity persistence.
No LLM client or agent analysis.
No real SerpApi, DataForSEO, Brave Search, or external HTTP calls.
No dashboard or Run Scan UI.
No auth, cron, deployment, or paid service setup.
